content: HFrEF guideline-directed medical therapy (GDMT) consists of four foundational drug classes: (1) Beta-blockers (carvedilol, metoprolol succinate, bisoprolol)—reduce mortality, start low and titrate slowly; (2) ARNI (sacubitril/valsartan) preferred over ACEi/ARB—PARADIGM-HF showed superior outcomes vs enalapril, reduce NT-proBNP, reverse remodeling; (3) MRA (spironolactone or eplerenone)—RALES and EMPHASIS-HF trials showed mortality benefit, monitor potassium; (4) SGLT2 inhibitors (dapagliflozin or empagliflozin)—DAPA-HF and EMPEROR-Reduced trials showed benefit regardless of diabetes status. Diuretics (loop diuretics like furosemide, torsemide, bumetanide) for symptom relief but no mortality benefit. Additional options: hydralazine/isosorbide dinitrate for Black patients with persistent symptoms (A-HeFT trial), ivabradine for HR ≥70 despite beta-blocker, digoxin for symptom control. Device therapy: ICD for primary prevention if EF ≤35% after ≥3 months GDMT, CRT if QRS ≥150ms with LBBB morphology.
